title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: U.S. Marshals arrested Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris in connection with the June 27 shooting on Blue Hill Avenue.
The Mattapan shooting resulted in one death and four injuries; charges include assault to murder and assault with a dangerous weapon.
Both men pleaded not guilty and are held in Suffolk County Jail on bails of $1 million and $250,000 respectively.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts reported 18 cases of cyclosporiasis from May 1 to July 1, aligning with normal seasonal levels.
Current outbreaks in the Midwest involve over 400 cases, but Massachusetts is unaffected, says DPH.
Cyclospora outbreaks are linked to contaminated produce; washing may not remove the parasite completely.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Courageous Sailing Center adjusted logistics to continue youth sailing programs despite Tall Ships docking at nearby Pier 4.
Piers Park Sailing Center safely managed sailing lessons on busy Boston Harbor with 23-foot Sonars during Sail Boston 250.
Community Boating Inc. engaged festival visitors in sailing education, integrating Tall Ships activities with regular programs.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Department of Environmental Protection issued an air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke.
The alert warns that air quality is unhealthy for sensitive groups like children and people with lung disease.
Residents are advised to limit outdoor activities and monitor air quality updates via MassDEP's MassAir map.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Oosterschelde, a historic Dutch tall ship, welcomed visitors at Rowes Wharf for Sail Boston 2026.
The 164-foot Oosterschelde, built in 1918, is the only remaining Dutch schooner from the early 20th century.
After Sail Boston, Oosterschelde will offer tours in Quincy before heading to Canada, Spain, and France.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room after a performance at the 2024 Outlaw Music Fest.
Dylan, impressed by Spencer's performance, later invited her to join his 2026 summer tour as an opening act.
Spencer will perform with Dylan on July 16 in Boston and July 18 in New Hampshire, along with Jimmie Vaughan.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Contessa at The Newbury Boston and Mooo Beacon Hill are top hotel restaurants in Boston, according to OpenTable.
Maine's top hotel restaurants include Earth at Hidden Pond and White Barn Inn Restaurant in Kennebunk.
Michael Jordan’s Steak House at Mohegan Sun, Uncasville, Conn., is also among the top hotel restaurants in New England.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović reflects on childhood memories of the World Cup starting with the 1982 tournament in Belgrade.
Mitrović recalls the 1990 World Cup, a memorable event that overshadowed his aunt's wedding due to Yugoslavia's match.
As Revolution's head coach, Mitrović shares pride in past roles, including Serbia's U20 triumph and coaching in US tournaments.

title: Joe Mazzulla showcased MMA training regimen, spoke about its influence on coaching the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla incorporates MMA, specifically Brazilian jiu-jitsu with Eric Nicksick, to enhance his coaching approach.
Mazzulla finds MMA training mentally and physically refreshing, likening its mindset to facing challenges in basketball coaching.
During his MMA regimen, Mazzulla studies communication strategies, applying lessons to in-game coaching of the Celtics.

title: Report: Wrong-way driver who killed trooper had 9 drinks before Route 1 crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Driver Hernan Ramon Marrero had a blood alcohol level of 0.192, over twice the legal limit, during the fatal crash.
Trooper Kevin Trainor died after colliding with Marrero's wrong-way vehicle on Route 1 on May 6.
Marrero consumed nine alcoholic drinks at Tribu Mexican Kitchen and Bar before driving the wrong way in Peabody.
